design_philosophy.core_loop: Place falling tetrominoes to clear lines and solve puzzle configurations, similar to Dr. Mario but with Tetris pieces instead of pills
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Managing piece placement to clear specific puzzle objectives within spatial constraints
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Mastering spatial reasoning and pattern recognition through classic Tetris mechanics adapted to puzzle-solving scenarios
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Tetris 2 deviates from pure Tetris by introducing Dr. Mario-style puzzle modes where specific objectives must be met rather than maximizing line clears. This positions it as a hybrid that emphasizes puzzle design philosophy over arcade-style score chasing.

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Multiple game modes available including Normal, Puzzle, and versus modes, suggesting adjustable challenge levels through mode selection

progression.unlock_mechanism: Round-based progression with cutscene/movie rewards for clearing rounds
replayability_hooks: Multiple game modes (Normal, Puzzle, 1P vs 2P, 1P vs COM)
Computer opponent difficulty selection (animal-themed skill levels: Turtle, Dog, Rabbit)

world_building_techniques: Round-clear movies that play between levels
Unused cutscenes suggest narrative framing (Baking, Boxing themes indicate character contexts)
Regional title presentation differences (animated with flashing in JP version, static in international)

faction_indicators: Versus modes (1P vs 2P, 1P vs COM) suggest competitive faction play
danger_signals: Cartridge copy detection via SRAM write attempts indicates piracy concerns
Region-locking error messages prevent unauthorized cartridge use
environmental_storytelling: Cartoon character opponents replace original NES animal-themed opponents
Cutscene themes (Baking, Boxing) suggest character-driven narrative contexts

sources_used: tcrf - Tetris 2 (SNES) The Cutting Room Floor
